columns.name: development_type
columns.description: Canonical label: New, Extension, Refurbishment, etc.

project_building_uses.description: Building-use tags per project (1:many). One row per (project, building-use category). project_fields.building_use_code is the denormalised primary — i.e. the is_primary = TRUE row here. RSM resolved via project_category_mappings → project_category_types → rsm_building_use_mapping; Opus via base_sf__projectcategory → base_sf__lu_category.ExternalId__c → opus_building_use_mapping (merged in silver; live after next ETL run). CW multi-tag silver still pending (see docs/building_use_spec.md § 8). See docs/gold_layer_specification.md § 4.

columns.name: project_building_use_id
columns.description: Deterministic XXHASH64(project_id, source category id). Primary key.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: FK to project_fields.project_id.

columns.name: building_use_code
columns.description: Hubexo gold building-use code (BU-N). building_use_group resolved via building_use_definitions join.

columns.name: source_building_use
columns.description: Original category label from the source system.

columns.name: is_primary
columns.description: TRUE for the project's primary tag — exactly one row per project (enforced by a singular test). RSM: main=1 with a lex tie-break on project_category_type_id ASC; Opus: lowest CategoryRankNumber__c with a lex tie-break on ExternalId__c ASC. Equals the row surfaced on project_fields.building_use_code.

columns.name: ranking
columns.description: 1 for the primary tag, 2+ for secondaries. Deterministic per source (RSM: main DESC, project_category_type_id ASC; Opus: CategoryRankNumber__c ASC, ExternalId__c ASC).

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: Source freshness for the tag. RSM: GREATEST of the project and category-mapping timestamps; Opus: the project's last_modified_at.

project_dimensions.description:
Cross-sector physical dimensions and counts per project (1:1 on
project_id). Every project gets a row, but most columns are sparse
(coverage typically 0-4 fields per project across the 16). Sector-
specific dimensions (project_residential / project_hospitality / …)
ship as separate 1:1 LEFT JOIN-able tables in later PRs.
See docs/dimensions_spec.md and docs/gold_layer_specification.md § 4.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: FK to project_fields.project_id.

columns.name: storeys_above_ground
columns.description: Maximum storey count above ground level. (PJ-14)

columns.name: storeys_below_ground
columns.description: Maximum basement/sub-grade storey count. (PJ-15)

columns.name: building_height_m
columns.description: Linear distance from ground to top floor, metres. (PJ-16) Opus only.

columns.name: gross_floor_area_sqm
columns.description: Total floor area summed across all floors, m². (PJ-18) CW source is sq ft, converted ×0.092903.

columns.name: gross_floor_area_source
columns.description: Provenance enum (Researcher / System Calculated / Estimated / Confirmed). (PJ-19) NULL today — no source surfaces this hint.

columns.name: site_area_sqm
columns.description: Land area of the project site, m². (PJ-20) Opus only (hectares × 10,000); NULL on CW + RSM.

columns.name: number_of_buildings
columns.description: Distinct building structures on the project. (PJ-21) RSM huskroppar, Opus structures, CW BuildingCount.

columns.name: above_ground_parking_spaces
columns.description: (PJ-22) RSM source conflates above + below ground; same caveat applies to CW ParkingCount.

columns.name: basement_parking_spaces
columns.description: (PJ-23) Opus only.

columns.name: bicycle_spaces
columns.description: (PJ-24) Opus only.

columns.name: refurbishment_area_sqm
columns.description: Area being rebuilt/refurbished, m². (PJ-25) RSM only. Only meaningful when development_type ∈ ('Refurbishment','Alteration/Addition/Conversion').

columns.name: gross_volume_cbm
columns.description: Total enclosed building volume, m³. (PJ-26) RSM only.

columns.name: demolition_area_sqm
columns.description: Area being demolished, m². (PJ-27) RSM only. Only meaningful when development_type = 'Demolition'.

columns.name: usable_floor_area_sqm
columns.description: Net floor area inside walls (bruksarea), m². (PJ-28) RSM only.

columns.name: building_footprint_sqm
columns.description: Footprint on the ground (byggnadsarea), m². (PJ-32) RSM only.

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: GREATEST of project + measurement source timestamps.

project_documents.description:
Linked documents / files per project (permits, drawings, RFPs, news,
developer pages, …). 1:many on project_id. Each row is a single link
with a controlled `document_type`. Sparse: only CW (report_url_static)
and Opus (ProjectUrl) carry any source today; ~12K CW projects have a
link (mostly drawings) and ~10.8K Opus projects carry a ProjectUrl on
the dev sample. RSM is deferred (no source). Storage is two-phase
(see docs/documents_spec.md § 3): Phase 1 (now) `url` is the original
external source link, reused as-is and mirrored to Lakebase
(project_documents_synced) — no PDF is hosted. Phase 2 (future) a
separate sync ingests the PDFs into Databricks-managed storage and
generates our own served link that lands in `url` with no schema
change.

columns.name: project_document_id
columns.description: Deterministic XXHASH64(project_id, origin_id). Primary key.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: FK to project_fields.project_id.

columns.name: document_type
columns.description: Gold document type code, e.g. `permit`, `drawing`, `rfp`, `news`, `developer_site`. Vocabulary in seeds/gold_document_types.csv.

columns.name: title
columns.description: Human-readable label for the document (NULL when the source carries none).

columns.name: description
columns.description: Optional longer description of the document.

columns.name: url
columns.description: Resolvable link to the document. Phase 1 (now): the original external source link, reused as-is (no PDF hosted by us). Phase 2 (future): our own served link to an owned PDF in Databricks-managed storage — same column, no schema change. See docs/documents_spec.md § 3.

columns.name: media_type
columns.description: MIME type inferred from the URL extension. `application/pdf` when detectable, else NULL.

columns.name: origin_id
columns.description: Source-native id of the link (CW UrlId; Opus origin_id) for traceability.

columns.name: origin_research_system
columns.description: `CW` | `OPUS`. Matches the project_metadata convention.

columns.name: sort_order
columns.description: Display order where the source provides one; NULL otherwise.

columns.name: is_visible
columns.description: Whether the link is publicly visible in the source (CW Display=1; Opus IsPublished).

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: Source link's last-modified timestamp; drives the incremental merge.

project_fields.description: Base table for a construction project. Uses the three-dimension stage model (planning_stage, contract_stage, project_status). See docs/gold_layer_specification.md § 5.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: project_id that links all Mimir project tables

columns.name: project_heading
columns.description: One line that summarises the work

columns.name: description
columns.description: Text elaborating project features, etc.

columns.name: planning_stage_id
columns.description: FK → gold_planning_stages.id. How far design / documentation has progressed (1=Early Planning … 7=Building Approval). NULL when project_status is frozen (Cancelled / On hold / Status pending / Site for Sale / Info Unavailable).

columns.name: contract_stage_id
columns.description: FK → gold_contract_stages.id. Where main contractor procurement is (1=Pre-Tender … 8=Completed). NULL when project_status is frozen.

columns.name: project_status_id
columns.description: FK → gold_project_statuses.id. Overall project health (1=In progress … 8=Info Unavailable). Always populated — never null.

columns.name: construction_start_date
columns.description: Start of main works

columns.name: construction_end_date
columns.description: When work is expected to end

columns.name: project_value
columns.description: Cost of construction in local currency, does not include planning or land acquisition cost

columns.name: currency
columns.description: Currency code per ISO 4217

columns.name: image_url
columns.description: (Bonus field) URL to an image of the project, if available (never)

columns.name: source_building_use
columns.description: Original project-type / sector label from the source system, retained for traceability. Opus: base_sf__lu_category.Name (English, e.g. 'Houses (PRS)'; equals the seed source_project_type_name). CW: source_project_type_name from seed (English, e.g. 'Retail'). RSM: project_category_types.name (English, e.g. 'Apartments').

columns.name: development_type_id
columns.description: FK → development_types.id. Resolve label via JOIN development_types (column development_type): New, Extension, Refurbishment, etc.

columns.name: contract_type
columns.description: Form of the main construction contract — `General/Traditional`, `Design Build`, `Construction Management`, `Developer Builder`, `Public Private Partnership`, `Framework`, `Term Contract`, `Engineering Procurement & Construction / Turnkey`, `Project Management`, `Local / In-house labour`, `Not specified / Not yet established`. Distinct from `contract_stage` (procurement timeline). Opus via mart_projects.ContractType → opus_contract_type_mapping; RSM via projects.contract_type_id → contract_types.

columns.name: ownership_type
columns.description: Sector funding/ownership category — `Public / Government`, `Private`, `Public Private Partnership`, `Other`. Opus via mart_projects.Funding (JSON array; primary = first element) → opus_ownership_type_mapping. RSM NULL today — per spec § 5.3 it derives from the developer company's classification via Company Registry, deferred until that dependency lands. CW NULL (no project-level bronze field). See docs/project_classification_spec.md § 5.1.

columns.name: ownership_level
columns.description: Sub-classification of ownership_type — `Foreign Aid / International`, `National / Federal`, `State`, `Local`, `Non-Profit`, `International`, `Housing Association`, `Blank / Not specified`. The (ownership_type, ownership_level) pair must be one of the valid combinations defined in docs/project_classification_spec.md § 5.2 (enforced by the singular pair-validity test).

columns.name: visibility
columns.description: Whether a consumer should show the project by default. `visible` = show normally; `hidden` = kept in gold but pulled from the public listing (a consumer following the project can still resolve it — downstream services own that logic). Projects that should never be shown at all (hard deletes, duplicates, drafts) are excluded from gold entirely upstream. Per-source derivation: RSM project_type_id=6 'Hidden, not public' → hidden; Opus IsArchived=TRUE → hidden; CW Display=0 → hidden. See docs/{rsm,o

columns.name: parent_project_id
columns.description: Global gold project_id of this project's parent, when the source links projects (e.g. phased / superseded projects). Soft reference — the parent may itself be hidden or excluded, so this is NOT a hard FK. Opus from mart_projects.ParentProjectIds (first element); CW from report_static.ParentReportId. RSM always NULL — bronze has no usable parent link. NULL = no parent. See docs/{rsm,opus,cw}_mapping.md § Parent project link.

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: The last time any of the fields in this table were modified. This is a bonus field that is not in the original Mimir model

project_green_building.description:
Green building / environmental certifications per project. 1:many
on project_id — a project can pursue multiple schemes (Opus
carries BREEAM + CEEQUAL + CCS independently; RSM averages ~1.8
schemes per certified project). Sparse: ~3-5% of any source's
projects carry an entry. Replaces the previously-disabled
green_building.sql. See docs/green_building_spec.md.

columns.name: project_green_building_id
columns.description: Deterministic XXHASH64(project_id, scheme_code). Primary key.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: FK to project_fields.project_id.

columns.name: scheme_code
columns.description: Hubexo gold scheme code, e.g. `breeam`, `ceequal`, `dgnb`, `miljobyggn`. 22 schemes in seeds/gold_green_building_schemes.csv.

columns.name: rating
columns.description: Scheme-specific text rating (BREEAM Pass/Good/Excellent/Outstanding; LEED Certified/Silver/Gold/Platinum; CEEQUAL Award etc.). NULL for RSM (tag-only) and CCS.

columns.name: score
columns.description: Numeric final score where the scheme defines one (BREEAM, CEEQUAL). NULL elsewhere.

columns.name: interim_score
columns.description: Pre-construction / interim score (CEEQUALInterimGrade).

columns.name: percentage
columns.description: Final percentage score where relevant (CEEQUAL).

columns.name: interim_percentage
columns.description: Interim percentage score (CEEQUAL).

columns.name: assessment_status
columns.description: Pre-assessment / Interim / Final / Certified / Registered. Drawn from BREEAMStage, CEEQUALStatus, or derived for CCS.

columns.name: scheme_version
columns.description: Version label of the scheme (BREEAMVersion, CEEQUALVersion).

columns.name: reference_number
columns.description: External reference: BREEAMReference, CEEQUALProjectCode, CCSregistrationId.

columns.name: certified_date
columns.description: Date the project was certified (BREEAMCertifiedDate, CEEQUALVerifiedDate).

columns.name: gb_project_value
columns.description: Project value as declared in the green-building data (CEEQUALValue).

columns.name: gb_currency
columns.description: Currency of the green-building project value (CEEQUALCurrencyISO).

columns.name: source_scheme_name
columns.description: Original scheme name from the source system, retained for traceability.

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: GREATEST of project + scheme-source timestamps.

project_materials.description:
Materials / components / work types per project. 1:many on
project_id — a project can carry many material rows. Resolved
against the Mimir Superset Materials catalogue (965 codes) via
per-source mapping seeds. See docs/materials_spec.md and
docs/gold_layer_specification.md § 4.

columns.name: project_material_id
columns.description: Deterministic XXHASH64(project_id, source_material_name). Primary key.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: FK to project_fields.project_id.

columns.name: material_code
columns.description: Hubexo gold material code (MAT-NNNN). NULL when source value isn't yet in the seed.

columns.name: material_group
columns.description: Coarse rollup. From seed when material_code resolves; falls back to source's own group label otherwise.

columns.name: source_material_name
columns.description: Original material name from the source system.

columns.name: source_kind
columns.description: Granularity discriminator: 'material' (Opus), 'trade' (RSM, future), 'csi' (CW, future), 'craft' (RMS, future). Consumers querying 'what is this built of' should filter to 'material'.

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: Timestamp of the latest update from source.

project_metadata.description: Metadata fields for a construction project. These are fields that are not directly about the project itself, but about the data we have on the project.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: project_id that links all Mimir project tables

columns.name: first_published_by
columns.description: The user that first published the project in source

columns.name: first_published_at
columns.description: When the project was first published in source

columns.name: latest_published_by
columns.description: The user that last published the project in source

columns.name: latest_published_at
columns.description: When the project was last published in source

columns.name: origin_research_system
columns.description: (Bonus field) The system from which the project data was sourced.

columns.name: origin_id
columns.description: (Bonus field) The original ID of the project in the source system.

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: The last time any of the fields in this table were modified. This is a bonus field that is not in the original Mimir model

project_residential.description:
Residential-sector dimension extension (1:1 on project_id, sparse —
only projects with residential unit data get a row, so LEFT JOIN it
onto project_dimensions / project_fields). Counts that only apply to
residential projects live here rather than on the cross-sector
project_dimensions core. total_unit_count (RES-1) is the only field
with a live source today (RSM "Number of apartments"); the Opus RES-*
fields (units started/completed, bedroom mix, affordable / care-home
/ student-bed counts) land in later PRs.
See docs/dimensions_spec.md § 4.2 and docs/gold_layer_specification.md § 4.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: FK to project_fields.project_id.

columns.name: total_unit_count
columns.description: Number of residential units (dwellings) in the project. (RES-1) RSM 'Number of apartments' today; also fed by APAC / CD21 'Residential units' when their bronze lands. A source 0 is treated as unknown → row is absent (never 0).

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: GREATEST of project + measurement source timestamps.

project_role_contacts.description: Named persons per project role assignment. Links each role assignment to contacts in the Company Registry. A role assignment can have multiple contacts (many:many).

columns.name: assignment_contact_id
columns.description: Primary key for this mapping table.

columns.name: assignment_id
columns.description: Foreign key to project_roles.assignment_id.

columns.name: contact_id
columns.description: Reference to the contact in the Company Registry. Used to resolve current email/phone details. Null if the contact cannot be matched.

columns.name: person_role_type
columns.description: The role type of the contact person in relation to the project role assignment.

project_roles.description: Project roles assigned to this workplace. A project can have multiple roles and multiple assignment per role

columns.name: assignment_id
columns.description: The unique assignment ID for this project role.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: project_id that links all Mimir project tables

columns.name: workplace_id
columns.description: The Company Registry workplace_id that is associated with this project role assignment. Note that this is not a required field, as some roles are assigned to companies without a specific workplace in RSM.

columns.name: company_id
columns.description: Company Registry ID of the company holding the role on the project. NULL for CW + Opus until the Company-Registry stitch lands — those rows are attributed via company_name only.

columns.name: company_name
columns.description: Denormalised legal name of the company holding the role. GOLD EXPLORER USE ONLY — do NOT consume in customer-facing apps or products; those must resolve the canonical company name through the Company Registry (via company_id / workplace_id), never off this column. Retained because CW + Opus carry company_id = NULL until the Company-Registry stitch lands, so it is their only company attribution today; RSM populates it from the Company Registry legal_name.

columns.name: project_role_code
columns.description: Hubexo gold role code (e.g. ARC-001, MCT-009), assigned to the company. Mapped from RSM role types via the rsm_role_mapping seed (see seeds/gold_role_definitions.csv and docs/roles_proposal.md). Unmapped roles default to OTH-006 (Enquiries).

columns.name: source_role_name
columns.description: The original RSM project role type name before gold-layer mapping

columns.name: last_modified_at
columns.description: Timestamp of the latest update from source.

site_address.description: Address related fields for a construction project.

columns.name: project_id
columns.description: project_id that links all Mimir project tables

columns.name: country
columns.description: The country where the project is located.

columns.name: admin_level_1
columns.description: The first level administrative division where the project is located. This is typically a state, province or region.

columns.name: admin_level_2
columns.description: The second level administrative division where the project is located. This is typically a county, district or municipality.

columns.name: latitude
columns.description: latitude of the project site, if available

columns.name: longitude
columns.description: longitude of the project site, if available
